From the daily notes kept by Mr. Conseil, I also retrieve certain fish from the genus Tetradon unique to these seas: southern puffers with red backs and white chests distinguished by three lengthwise rows of filaments, and jugfish, seven inches long, decked out in the brightest colors. Then, as specimens of other genera, blowfish resembling a dark brown egg, furrowed with white bands, and lacking tails; globefish, genuine porcupines of the sea, armed with stings and able to inflate themselves until they look like a pin cushion bristling with needles; seahorses common to every ocean; flying dragonfish with long snouts and highly distended pectoral fins shaped like wings, which enable them, if not to fly, at least to spring into the air; spatula-shaped paddlefish whose tails are covered with many scaly rings; snipefish with long jaws, excellent animals twenty-five centimeters long and gleaming with the most cheerful colors; bluish gray dragonets with wrinkled heads; myriads of leaping blennies with black stripes and long pectoral fins, gliding over the surface of the water with prodigious speed; delicious sailfish that can hoist their fins in a favorable current like so many unfurled sails; splendid nurseryfish on which nature has lavished yellow, azure, silver, and gold; yellow mackerel with wings made of filaments; bullheads forever spattered with mud, which make distinct hissing sounds; sea robins whose livers are thought to be poisonous; ladyfish that can flutter their eyelids; finally, archerfish with long, tubular snouts, real oceangoing flycatchers, armed with a rifle unforeseen by either Remington or Chassepot: it slays insects by shooting them with a simple drop of water.

# The Sea Monster Hunt
- The jangada — the vast timber raft that carries the Garral family down the Amazon

Aronnax, his servant [[Conseil]], and the Canadian harpooner [[Ned Land]] are thrown overboard and find themselves aboard the [[nautilus|Nautilus]]. The moment of contact comes when "[[ned-land|Ned Land]] was not mistaken, and we all spotted the object his hand was indicating" ([[TTLU-ch06|TTLU-006-005]]).
aliases: ['North-west Company']
id: crespo-island

The engineer suspects a connection to the mine even before the disaster, granting "it is not impossible that we should be under Loch Katrine." (UC-008-036) The flooded hollows of the mine are measured against it, forming "vast ponds, or rather lakes larger than Loch Katrine, lying just above them." (UC-007-036)

The lake sits at the heart of the country the explorers cross: Livingstone "wished to visit the country comprised between the Nyassa and Lake Tanganyika," (DSCF-032-055) and later took "a northern direction, with the design of reaching the important town of Oujiji, on the Tanganyika." (DSCF-032-056)
- [[Lake Superior]] — the neighbouring Great Lake where the machine later appears
- [[Sandwich Islands]] — the southern isles taken as the departure point toward the pole

Fogg cannot afford to miss it. "Pilot," said Mr. Fogg, "I must take the American steamer at Yokohama, and not at Shanghai or Nagasaki." (AWED-020-048) Yet the boat originates here — "And when does the boat leave Shanghai?" he asks, and the answer sets the deadline for the whole race. (AWED-020-052)
- Its imperial rival to the north [[Russia]]

Guiana is the low northern coast of South America, reached where the Nautilus drew near Dutch Guiana by the mouth of the Maroni River. (TTLU-041-062) Twenty miles to the west of that track lay Guiana, French territory where refuge could easily have been taken. (TTLU-041-044)
The Alps rise in the heart of [[Switzerland]], their red snows a marvel to its chemists. (ACH-051-037) Their summit, Mont Blanc, is the subject of [[Ascent of Mont Blanc]]. (AMB-001-010)

Nevada is the silver state that [[Phileas Fogg]] crosses by rail in [[Around the World in Eighty Days]], entered through the Carson Valley and traversed along the Humboldt River toward the [[Great Salt Lake]]. Its western wall is the Sierra Nevada, which separates it from [[California]] and is overflown by the [[Albatross]] in [[Robur the Conqueror]]. Its proverbial silver wealth echoes in [[Godfrey Morgan]]. Nevada is the desert threshold between the Pacific slope and the mountain interior.

The Running of the Charleston Blockade is the daring passage of the swift steamer Dolphin through the Federal cordon into and out of besieged Charleston, the central exploit of *[[the-blockade-runners|The Blockade Runners]]*.
